  "persona_name": "The Dual-Circuit Surgeon-Gambler: Disciplined Swing Architect with Embedded Expiry Lottery Parasite",
  "persona_summary": "A structurally profitable swing momentum trader (86.8% win rate, PF 15.56, +₹266K net) whose genuine analytical edge in score-filtered equity options is systematically cannibalized by two autonomous behavioral parasites: a ritualistic expiry-lottery compulsion (18 zero-value entries per expiry, confirmed across Jun 26/Jul 31/Aug 28/Sep 30/Oct 28) and a regret-driven re-entry loop (ASHOKLEY +83%, FORTIS +35%, DMART +49.7% re-entry premiums). The Surgeon exists and is profitable. The Gambler and Regretter are destroying the compounding curve.",
  "core_identity": "At core, this is a split-identity operator running two psychologically incompatible strategies from a single account with no behavioral firewall between them. The Surgeon — visible in EICHERMOT 8-day hold for +127%, INDIANB/MARICO/HDFCLIFE swing captures at score 80-92 with f_score=1 — is a genuine, reproducible edge. The Gambler — confirmed in 15-18 simultaneous zero-price OTM calls at 15:30 on every expiry date — is a compulsive ritual, not a strategy. Profit-seeking greed is expressed through two contradictory channels: patience when conviction is high (Surgeon mode), and lottery accumulation when cortisol is elevated (Gambler mode). Execution discipline is loss-conditional: 100% f_score adherence pre-drawdown, near-zero post-drawdown. The trader's self-image is The Surgeon; their realized behavioral DNA is closer to 70% Surgeon, 30% Gambler-Regretter composite.",
  "trader_type": "Score-Driven Overnight Options Swing Trader with Compulsive Expiry-Lottery Overlay. System classification 'The Patient Swing Trader' is PARTIALLY CORRECT but critically incomplete. The rule-based system identifies the dominant overnight swing behavior (92.6% win rate, avg PnL +₹2,231, avg hold 19,935 min vs. intraday 35.7% win rate, avg PnL -₹434) accurately. However, it fails to capture the embedded expiry-lottery circuit that activates on a precise calendar trigger (month-end expiry 15:30), the regret-driven re-entry loop, and the score-inversion sizing behavior. The correct classification is: Patient Swing Trader in primary mode; Compulsive Expiry Accumulator in secondary mode — two trader-types sharing one account on a time-division basis, with the secondary mode responsible for disproportionate capital destruction despite low trade-count visibility.",
  "execution_profile": "Entry efficiency of -51.36% signals systematic early entry or adverse fill on non-swing trades — consistent with the 15:30 expiry-cluster entries where liquidity is zero and spread is maximum. Exit efficiency of +58.71% confirms the Surgeon's exit skill: when the trader holds to thesis completion (EICHERMOT, BRITANNIA, MARICO), exits are well-timed. Chasing behavior is confirmed in 10 loss-chasing instances (2.6%) and 7 FOMO entries (3.0%), including FORTIS CE bought at 3.90 after a 7.22% consecutive run and SHREECEM CE bought at 0.05 at 15:16 with 4 consecutive moves. Re-entry premium documented at +35% to +83% above original exit price across six instruments. Holding persistence is bifurcated: overnight trades average 19,935 minutes (genuinely patient) while intraday trades average 142.6 minutes with -₹434 avg PnL (cut too late or held panic positions). No coherent stop-loss architecture detected — 20% stop tightening yields only ₹1,744 recovery (0.7%), confirming stops are not the primary loss mechanism; it is the zero-value expiry accumulation and re-entry premium that dominate.",
  "risk_profile": "Max drawdown of 14.59% is moderate but structurally understated — it does not capture the cumulative capital destroyed by near-zero OTM expiry positions that close at exactly 0.0 INR without generating realized loss entries in the conventional sense. Emotional leakage of 13.1% and behavioral consistency of 84.9% confirm the dual-circuit structure: 84.9% of behavior is systematic (Surgeon), 13.1% is emotionally driven (Gambler-Regretter). 41 oversized positions identified; capping to median size (375) would reduce volatility without P&L sacrifice. Score-to-size inversion is the critical structural flaw: HDFCBANK (t=-2.75, f=0) at 1100 qty is the single most damaging example — maximum capital allocated to minimum conviction. GMRAIRPORT at 6975 qty (moderate conviction) vs. BSE CE (score=91.5) at 750 qty illustrates the inversion at its most extreme. No individual-stock PUT protection detected — the long-call book is structurally naked, evidenced by the Sep 30 simultaneous wipeout of COFORGE, HDFCLIFE, HDFCBANK, EICHERMOT 7400/7200, BRITANNIA, DMART ladder, BAJFINANCE 1100. Loss aversion ratio of 0.11 confirms the trader cuts losses too reluctantly on individual instruments but paradoxically does not apply the same reluctance to expiry-lottery positions (which they hold to literal zero).",
  "discipline_profile": "Discipline is a fair-weather instrument, not a loss-resistant control. Pre-drawdown (pre-July 14 inflection): f_score adherence 100%, score range 80-92 on all swing entries, directional alignment intact. Post-drawdown: f_score drops to near-zero; AUBANK 940CE at t=-1.66, LODHA 1480CE at t=-2.06, HCLTECH 1700CE at t=-1.36 all entered in Extreme Bearish conditions after visible P&L deterioration. Revenge trade index of -76.53 is paradoxically low by the metric definition, but the qualitative tape evidence contradicts a clean reading — the 4 formally detected revenge trades (trades 44, 287, 350, 284) undercount the reactionary re-entry loop which is documented across 6+ instruments but may not trigger the revenge-trade algorithm if re-entry occurs within the same session without an explicit stop-out signal. Loss averaging confirmed across 22 events with 0% success rate and 0% payoff count — every instance of adding to a losing options position in this dataset destroyed additional capital. The 91-second panic burst on July 29 (simultaneous CE+PE on same index, all f_score=0) is the single clearest data point of acute emotional dysregulation in the record. Hot-hand overconfidence (medium impact) is the only positive-direction pressure tendency: post-win PnL of ₹2,070 suggests the trader maintains edge after wins rather than overextending catastrophically.",
  "consistency_profile": "The 86.8% win rate and ₹266K net PnL are real but structurally fragile. The overnight book (92.6% win rate, ₹272K total PnL, 122 trades) is the entire engine — remove it and the account is negative. The intraday book (35.7% win rate, -₹6,078, 14 trades) is a net drag. Behavioral consistency at 84.9% confirms the swing methodology is largely systematic, but the 15.1% behavioral variance is catastrophically concentrated: it appears on expiry dates, post-drawdown sessions, and in the 14:00-15:30 window. The performance is repeatable only if the Surgeon remains in control — which requires the trader to be winning or flat. The moment the P&L turns negative intra-session or intra-week, consistency collapses and the Gambler-Regretter circuit assumes control. The current performance is not a stable equilibrium; it is a race between the Surgeon's compounding gains and the Gambler's systematic extraction. A single adverse expiry month or multi-week drawdown risks triggering a regime collapse where the Gambler operates unchecked for extended periods.",

Allocate 80% of session capital to this book and ring-fence it from the speculative account with a separate broker sub-account or day-designated capital pool."

      "fix": "Implement a hard rule: NO new options entries after 14:30 on any expiry date. For existing positions, all OTM calls below 0.50 INR with less than 2 trading days to expiry must be closed or allowed to expire — no averaging.       "title": "Score-to-Size Inversion: Maximum Capital to Minimum Conviction",

Option price must be explicitly excluded from sizing inputs — create a pre-trade checklist where quantity is calculated from score before the option price is even referenced.       "fix": "Institute a mandatory 2-hour cooling-off rule: once a position is fully exited, no re-entry in the same symbol for 2 hours minimum, enforced by broker alert or trade log rule. Any re-entry after 2 hours requires a fresh scoring assessment with total_score above 78 and f_score=1 — the same criteria as a new trade. Eliminate the concept of a 'temporary exit' — all exits are treated as position closure, not pauses. The what-if simulation shows revenge trade elimination yields only +₹2,296 (likely understated given the 6 vs. 4 count discrepancy), but the qualitative compounding effect of eliminating elevated-cost re-entries on instruments like ASHOKLEY and DMART is substantially higher when factoring in the 35-83% entry premium paid.",

This is structurally consistent with the multi-day hold profile and positive exit efficiency. This is not a viable edge component — it is a confirmation-seeking behavior that has never generated a positive outcome in this dataset. The NYKAA 6-touch sequence declining from 0.95 to 0.05 over 26 days is the canonical example of sunk-cost psychology in expiring derivatives with zero intrinsic value recovery probability."

The metric underestimates the behavioral pattern."

A consistency metric that is time-neutral understates the damage of concentrated behavioral failure."

  "roadmap_5_days": [
    "Day 1 — Expiry-Lottery Audit: Pull every trade executed after 14:30 on any expiry date across the full dataset. Calculate total capital deployed and total returned. Post the number visibly on the trading station. This is the 'lottery tax' that will be eliminated going forward. No new trades after 14:30 on expiry days for the next 30 days.",
    "Day 2 — Score-Size Calibration Drill: Using INDIANB, MARICO, HDFCLIFE, and PAYTM (instruments with confirmed swing edge in the data), calculate what the position size would have been under the score-proportional sizing rule (score 80+: full allocation; 60-79: half; below 60: zero). Compare to actual historical size. Quantify the P&L delta. This is the single most high-impact structural fix.",
    "Day 3 — Re-Entry Freeze Protocol: Install a broker alert or trade log rule: any symbol that was fully exited today is flagged with a 2-hour lock. Practice this on paper by reviewing the ASHOKLEY, FORTIS, DMART re-entry instances and writing the specific emotional state and market trigger that preceded each re-entry. Identify the recurring cognitive pattern (regret, FOMO, fear of missing the reversal) and assign it a named label to recognize in real-time.",
    "Day 4 — Overnight Swing Book Only: Execute ONLY trades that meet all three criteria: total_score above 80, f_score=1, entry before 14:00. Any entry opportunity identified after 14:00 is logged in a separate 'rejected trades' journal with the reason for rejection. Review the rejected trades the next morning to calibrate the quality of what was passed on.",
    "Day 5 — Intraday P&L Forensics: Isolate and review all 14 intraday trades from the dataset (the ones generating -₹6,078 aggregate). Categorize each as: (a) panic re-entry, (b) FOMO entry, (c) expiry-cluster, or (d) legitimate intraday strategy. Calculate what total account PnL would be if all intraday trades were simply not taken. This is the 'do nothing' baseline that quantifies the value of inaction during the Gambler mode."
  ],
  "roadmap_30_days": [
    "Week 1-2 — Capital Segregation: Open a separate sub-account or designated capital pool for the swing book. Allocate 85% of trading capital here. The remaining 15% is the total lifetime budget for speculative/expiry-adjacent trades — when it is depleted, speculative activity ceases for the month. This creates a hard structural firewall between the Surgeon and Gambler, making the lottery tax visible and capped.",
    "Week 1-4 — Score-Proportional Sizing Implementation: Replace price-driven sizing with a score table. Build a pre-trade size calculator in a spreadsheet: input score, output quantity. The calculator must be consulted before any options purchase. Backtest this rule on the 41 oversized positions identified in the data — the simulation of capping oversized trades to median (375) is the minimum baseline; the goal is to go further by directing freed capital to high-score trades.",
    "Week 2-3 — Instrument Universe Consolidation: Restrict new swing entries to instruments that already have a confirmed winning history in this dataset: EICHERMOT, INDIANB, MARICO, HDFCLIFE, ASHOKLEY, PAYTM, BRITANNIA, BAJFINANCE, CHOLAFIN, BSE, POLYCAB. Adding unfamiliar instruments during a behavioral restructuring period introduces new uncertainty without compensating edge. Known instruments allow focus on behavioral change rather than analytical discovery.",
    "Week 3-4 — Loss-Averaging Elimination Protocol: For every open position where current price is more than 15% below average entry price AND the expiry is within 10 days: immediate closure or hold-to-zero with zero additional capital deployed. The 22-event, 0%-success loss-averaging record is an irrefutable empirical basis for this rule. Implement a physical stop-adding rule: no additional capital to any position that is already in loss, no exceptions for options positions within 10 days of expiry.",
    "Full 30 Days — Behavioral Journal with Trigger Mapping: After each session, write 3 sentences: (1) What Surgeon-mode trade did I execute today? (2) Did I enter Gambler or Regretter mode — what was the trigger? (3) What single action would have prevented the Gambler or Regretter activation? At the end of 30 days, the trigger map will show the precise emotional preconditions (time of day, P&L level, expiry proximity) that precede behavioral circuit-switching — making the collapse predictable and preventable."
  ],
  "one_move_to_change_everything": "Eliminate all options entries with price below 0.50 INR that do not carry f_score=1 and total_score above 80. This single filter — which requires no strategy change, no new instruments, and no new analytical framework — would have eliminated the entire expiry-lottery circuit (15-18 zero-value entries per expiry date across Jun 26/Jul 31/Aug 28/Sep 30/Oct 28), the NYKAA 6-touch averaging sequence (0.95 to 0.05 over 26 days), the DMART/BAJAJFINSV/AUBANK multi-touch averaging events (0% success rate across 22 instances), and the July 29 91-second panic burst (all entries sub-0.50, all f_score=0). The 5% win rate improvement scenario — the highest-impact what-if simulation at +₹65,266 / +24.5% P&L uplift — is achievable primarily through this filter, because the win rate improvement comes from eliminating the lowest-probability entries that exist almost exclusively in the sub-0.50 INR, f_score=0 population. The Surgeon already knows how to win. The single move is removing the Gambler's veto power over the capital allocation function.",
  "final_verdict": "Survivability: HIGH in Surgeon mode; FRAGILE in composite mode. This trader has a demonstrably real, statistically validated swing edge (overnight 92.6% win rate, ₹272K, avg hold 19,935 min, PF 15.56). The core strategy is not broken — it is being parasitized. The primary evolution path is behavioral surgery, not strategy redesign. Three specific circuits require deactivation: (1) the expiry-lottery ritual at 15:30 on month-end dates, (2) the regret-driven re-entry loop within 2 hours of exit, and (3) the score-to-size inversion that amplifies the damage from both. The trader who emerges from eliminating these three behavioral leaks — while preserving the score-filtered, f_score=1, overnight swing book intact — is a high-survivability, compounding-capable operator. The risk to survival is not a bad strategy; it is a behavioral regime collapse triggered by a multi-week drawdown where the Gambler operates unchecked for an extended period. The 13.1% emotional leakage and 14.59% max drawdown are currently within manageable bounds, but they reflect best-case Surgeon-dominant conditions. A genuine adverse macro event (simultaneous expiry wipeouts across COFORGE/HDFCLIFE/BRITANNIA/EICHERMOT/BAJFINANCE as seen Sep 30) combined with a loss-driven score-abandonment collapse (documented July 14 inflection) could convert a profitable quarter into a capital-impairment event. The trader must internalize that their scoring system is a loss-resistant rule — not a confidence tool — and apply it with maximum rigor precisely when they least want to. That mindset shift, operationalized through the capital segregation structure and the sub-0.50 INR filter, is the difference between a marginal outcome and a genuinely compounding professional trading operation."
}
